titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Tocqueville Family Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 10, 2025 at 23:00

did.unittitle: Tocqueville family papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In French, with a small amount of material in English.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notes, ledgers, government documents, and other papers relating to Alexis de Tocqueville, his friend and collaborator Gustave de Beaumont, and his father Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bernard Quaritch on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Alexis de Tocqueville Papers, 1835-1864, undated. II. Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville Papers, 1782-1842, undated. III. Other Papers, 1723-1973.

bioghist.head: Alexis de Tocqueville (1805-1859)
bioghist.p: Alexis de Tocqueville was a French politician and author best known for De la Démocratie en Amérique (1835, 1840). He traveled to the United States with his friend Gustave de Beaumont in 1831 after receiving a commission from King Louis-Phillipe to study the American penitentiary system. After nine months, they returned to France and published Du Système Pénitentiare aux Etats-Unis, et de son Application en France (two volumes, 1833). Tocqueville married Mary Mottley (1799-1864) in 1835.

bioghist.head: Hervé de Tocqueville (1772-1856)
bioghist.p: Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville was a French politician and the father of Alexis de Tocqueville. He served as prefect for the departments of Maine-et-Loire (1814-1815), Oise (1815-1816), Côte-d'Or (1816-1817), Moselle (1817-1824), the Somme (1823-1826), and Seine-et-Oise (1826-1828). He was also the author of Coup d'œil sur le règne de Louis XVI , first published in 1850.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tocqueville Family Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Most folder titles appearing in the contents list below were transcribed from the recto or verso of the artwork. Otherwise, folder titles were supplied by staff during processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notes, ledgers, government documents, and other papers relating to Alexis de Tocqueville, his friend and collaborator Gustave de Beaumont, and his father Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville. Correspondence includes a draft letter from Alexis de Tocqueville to John Stuart Mill, and letters to Tocqueville from Louis de Loménie, Richard Monckton Milnes, and Nassau William Senior. Also present are materials concerning the Tocqueville chateau and estate, including correspondence of Alexis de Tocqueville with lawyers and tradespeople, notes, and drawings of fences and gates. Writings contain an autograph manuscript copy of several volumes from Beaumont's edition of Œuvres complètes d'Alexis de Tocqueville (9 volumes, 1864-1867), including Tocqueville's works on travels in the United States, England, and Ireland; Beaumont's notes on reviews of De la Démocratie en Amérique in the French and foreign press; and Mary Mottley Tocqueville's notes on a manuscript relating to Thomas Robert Bugeaud. Papers of Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville consist of volumes of reports, speeches, bills, and other papers relating to his work as a prefect and member of the Chambre des Pairs, and drafts of writings such as Coup d'œil sur le règne de Louis XVI and "Des causes de la révolution du 20 mars et du retour de Bonaparte." The collection further contains 20th century materials concerning publication of Tocqueville's works by editor J. P. Mayer and photographs of the Tocqueville family estate.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Tocqueville Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Tocqueville, Alexis de, 1805-1859 (De la démocratie en Amérique)

did.unittitle: Alexis de Tocqueville Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers and correspondence of Alexis de Tocqueville and his writing partner Gustave de Beaumont. Included are letters to Tocqueville from Louis de Loménie, Richard Monckton Milnes, and Nassau William Senior, and a draft letter from Tocqueville to John Stuart Mill. The series contains autograph manuscript drafts from several volumes of Œuvres complètes d'Alexis de Tocqueville , Beaumont's edition of posthumously published Tocqueville works, as well as his collection of reviews of Democracy in America . Also included are a small amount of notes by Mary Mottley, Tocqueville's wife, and correspondence, notes, drawings, and other papers concerning the Tocqueville family chateau and estate.

did.unittitle: Beaumont, Clementine de. Notes de Clementine [on De la démocratie en Amérique ?]
Beaumont, Gustave de
"Beaumont Bibliographie des articles et revues sur La démocratie en Amérique "
"Notes - Voyage aux Estats-Unis," autograph manuscript
"Voyage en Angleterre, 1833," autograph manuscript
"Voyage en Irland, 1835," autograph manuscript
"Château de Tocqueville," letters and papers related to the Tocqueville chateau and estate
Correspondence
Loménie, Louis de to Alexis de Tocqueville
Milnes, Richard Monckton to Alexis de Tocqueville
Rozoy [first name unknown] to Alexis de Tocqueville
Senior, Minnie to Gustave Beaumont
Senior, Nassau William to Alexis de Tocqueville
Tocqueville, Alexis de to John Stuart Mill [draft]
Tocqueville, Alexis de to Mme. Romilly
Unidentified [H. Receur?]
Mottley, Mary. "Manuscript de Mr. Senior," autograph manuscript notes in English
"Notes de M. Alexis de Tocqueville relativement de à l'aménagement de Tocqueville [estate] spécialement à la bibliothèque -- adresses et prospectus de fournisseurs"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, lists, letters, drawings, plans, printed material, and other papers related to the Tocqueville chateau and estate.

did.unittitle: "Various letters" wrapper and notes regarding Tocqueville correspondence
Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials relating to Hervé Clérel de Tocqueville's career as a government official in France, writings by Tocqueville, several volumes recording rents collected on the family's estate, and a small amount of correspondence.

title.part: Coup d'œil sur le règne de Louis XVI

did.unittitle: "De la charte provinciale"
"Des causes de la révolution du 20 mars et du retour de Bonaparte"
Letter to Monsiuer Peuillet, librarian of the Institute
"Lettres de Monsieur de Tocqueville année 1817" [to Noël Dumarais]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an engraving of Alexis de Tocqueville.

did.unittitle: Parliamentary and administrative papers
Parliamentary and administrative papers
Pièces diverses, relatives à l'administration de M. le Comte de Tocqueville dans le département de la Côte d'Or
Pièces relatives à l'administration de M. le Comte de Tocqueville, comme préfet du département de la Moselle
Prefecture nominations
Recueil de discours, proclamations et mémoires, rédigés par le comte de Tocqueville pendant son administration dans les départements de Maine et Loire, de l'Oise et de la Côte d'Or
Recueil de mémoires, rapports, discours, etc. sur des objets politiques et d'administration… Départements de la Moselle, de la Somme et de Seine et Vise
Rent book
Rent book
"Tableau Comparatif des divers systèmes d'Administration Communale avant et depuis 1789"
"Tableau Comparatif des divers systèmes d'Administration Provinciale et Departmentale avant et depuis 1789"
Other Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, agreements, notes, and other materials concerning Jacob-Peter Mayer's publication of Tocqueville's Oeuvres Complètes (27 volumes, 1951-1983), printed material, and photographs of the Tocqueville family chateau. Correspondents include Count Jean de Tocqueville. The series additionally contains an 18th century document that may relate to the family or its property.

did.unittitle: Bibliothèque nationale (France). Hommage à Alexis de Tocqueville : 1805-1859 : manuscrits, portraits, souvenirs
Document signed by Clarendon and Robert Perrott
Hermann [first name unknown]. Letters to Antoine Revier and unknown recipients
Mayer, J. P.
"Alexis de Tocqueville and John Stuart Mill"
Letters from Count Jean de Tocqueville

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings, notes, and a few letters from others.

did.unittitle: Papers relating to Mayer's project to edit and publish Tocqueville's works
Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images of artwork, unidentified people, and the family estate.

did.unittitle: Simon, G. A.

title.part: Les Clarel á l'époque de la conquête d'Angleterre : et leur descendance dans la famille Clerel de Tocqueville

Histoire généalogique des Clérel, seigneurs de Rampan Tocqueville, Clouay, Lignerolles, Scey, Montfiquet, Sortosville, Montfarville, etc., barons de Boisnormand et des Bottereaux, comtes de Tocqueville, Normandie, 1066-1954
